title: Compliance Officer

rationales.taskAutomation: Regulatory-change monitoring, checklist reviews, and template policy drafting are largely automatable, but interpreting novel regulation and building compliance culture stay human-gated.
rationales.toolMaturity: RegTech platforms for reg-change tracking and policy drafting are credible and improving fast but not yet uniformly embedded across compliance functions.
rationales.adoption: Common at large regulated firms (finance, healthcare); adoption lags in smaller and less-regulated organizations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Monitoring regulatory feeds and tracking training completion are agent-ownable back-office slices; regulatory judgment calls are not.

tasksGrowing: AI governance and ethical AI policy development
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1041 (Compliance Officers), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI regulation is exploding worldwide, and monitoring/checklist work is automating fast, but every one of those new rules needs a human who can interpret it and build the internal case for compliance. AI-governance specialists are the fastest-growing niche in the field.
